text.title: Agent Observability Stack Blueprint Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of agent-observability-stack-blueprint enforces. Each rule has a statement, a rationale anchored to industry practice, and a source citation.

rule.statement: Stack must emit OTel spans for every LLM call, tool call, and retrieval call.
rule.rationale: Without OTel, cross-vendor / cross-provider correlation is impossible; debugging takes days instead of minutes.

rule.statement: Each request has a cost ledger entry: provider, model, input tokens, output tokens, USD.
rule.rationale: Cost is invisible without per-request tagging; bill-shock incidents are unattributable.

rule.statement: LLM-judge runs on a sampled fraction of production traffic (1-10%) with a static rubric.
rule.rationale: 100% judging doubles cost; 0% leaves silent drift. 1-10% catches drift within 1 week at 1k QPS.

rule.statement: Drift dashboard tracks p50/p95 of judge score over a 7-day rolling window; alert if 7-day drops by ≥0.1 vs baseline.
rule.rationale: Single-day drops are noise; 7-day rolling smooths and surfaces real regressions.

rule.statement: Vendor pick follows the decision tree: self-host data + open-source budget → Langfuse; LangChain heavy → LangSmith; gateway/cost-first → Helicone; ML-team integration → Phoenix.
rule.rationale: Forcing-fit a vendor against the wrong axis is the most common observability mistake in 2026.

rule.statement: Stub rule for conclusion 'skip-methodology' referenced from 06-decision-tree.xml. Replace with the real testable rule that resolves this branch of the tree.
rule.rationale: Auto-added by fix-methodology-phase-d.py to keep tree refs resolvable.
